title: Bad Bunny's Post-Grammy and Super Bowl Streaming Surge
text: Bad Bunny experienced unprecedented streaming growth following his Grammy Album of the Year win and subsequent Super Bowl LX halftime show performance. The artist had his second-largest streaming day ever on February 9, the day after his Super Bowl appearance, earning 98 million official on-demand U.S. streams, representing a 27% increase over Super Bowl day itself. Following the Super Bowl performance, Bad Bunny saw a 470 percent increase in Spotify streams in the U.S. and a 210 percent increase in global streams. His halftime show received 4.157 billion views in the first 24 hours across global and U.S. broadcasts, YouTube, and other digital platforms, making it the single most-watched Super Bowl halftime performance of all time. Bad Bunny made history as the first artist to headline the Super Bowl halftime show in Spanish, performing tracks from Debí Tirar Más Fotos which had just been honored as Album of the Year one week earlier. The combined momentum of the Grammy win and Super Bowl performance cemented Bad Bunny's position as one of the biggest music stars globally.
